evals.prompt: I have a new feature request 'Two-factor authentication' to add to TaskFlow. Create it with acceptance criteria, estimate 5 points, label it type/feature + area/auth, and drop it into the current cycle 'Sprint 15'. Show me the exact MCP tool calls.
evals.expected_output: Sequence of concrete tool calls with exact parameter names: list_projects → list_labels → list_cycles → create_work_item (with point, label_ids, description_html containing Given/When/Then AC) → add_work_items_to_cycle (with issue_ids array).
expectations: Lists labels before creating (to resolve label names → IDs)
Uses description_html with Given/When/Then format, not raw text
Passes point: 5 and label_ids as an array
Uses add_work_items_to_cycle with issue_ids (plural array, not issue_id)
Resolves cycle by name from list_cycles (not assumed)
References tool-patterns.md for exact parameter formats

evals.prompt: Walk me through the full sprint lifecycle from planning to close in Plane. Read the sprint-lifecycle scenario reference and show me the tool calls at each phase.
evals.expected_output: Opens references/scenarios/sprint-lifecycle.md, extracts each phase (planning, execution, standup, close, retro), and lists the concrete MCP tool calls from the scenario.
expectations: Names all key phases: planning, execution, standup, close, retro
Shows concrete tool calls (list_archived_cycles, create_cycle, add_work_items_to_cycle, etc.), not just prose
Links to dedicated skills (sprint-planning, daily-standup, sprint-review-retro) for deep-dives

evals.prompt: I need to publish a sprint report as a Plane page but last time my bullet list rendered broken. What's the correct HTML format?
evals.expected_output: Points to page-formatting.md, explains the list gotchas (nested list must start with text, no empty li, no data-checked).
expectations: Explicitly names the 'li must start with text not nested list' gotcha
Warns against data-checked attributes (stripped by Plane)

evals.prompt: What's a realistic workflow for linking a PR to a work item and moving it to review?
evals.expected_output: Sequence using /link add → /work-item update state → /comment → /assign, from everyday-commands.md.
expectations: Mentions that PR linking enables release notes generation
Uses the new command shortcuts (/link, /comment, /assign) not raw MCP
